channel.title: Comments for page "After Destruction"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "After Destruction"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If I may ask: what did you like? What left you feeling flat? What would you have done differently?

Well, … most of it left me feeling flat. The romanticization, the suicide, the use of Jungian archetype, the plotline, the use of PTSD symptoms - 'dead inside', 'something just… gone from their eyes', and references to commonly known genocides… the way you use all these elements is pretty generic.

I did like that the narrator really believed in what they were doing at the Foundation, and I liked the reflections on what humanity lost (the specific examples helped). And I like the first line, even though I absolutely don't like the suicide. Too obvious in the way it's done.

EDIT: As for the writing itself, I didn't feel an impact either (minus the opening lines). I would agree with Clef's critique, though I was less cognizant of those factors than he was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How do you know I'm not her fiancé?

What a twist!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well… The only one right now…

And the next will be the Anti-343?

P.S: Is a refence of Monitor and Anti-Monitor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I admit that the overall story is a bit… "In the mold", it's a good mold to fill, and quite competently done. Definitely an enjoyable read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By the way, 343 is the only reasonable candidate for a male reality bender attemptign to preserve the status qou.

Well… The only one right now…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
YOU DISGUSTING SACK OF CRAP, YOU HAD BEST UN-FUCK YOURSELF BEFORE I RIP OFF YOUR HEAD AND SHIT DOWN YOUR NECK!!!

… Sorry. Instinctive R. Lee Ermey reaction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those filthy piles of flesh and cloth are the most abhorrent, disgusting things in all of existence.. But at least they ARE things. The nothingness would obliterate me as well, and they are but a barrier to be kept up.

item.title: Re: Eerie but effective
item.content:encoded:
http://www.scp-wiki.net/project-heimdall
Still there, kind of stalled shortly after launch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this goes really well with the tale "meta-fiction" (I think that's the title, the one where the Foundation staffers realize they are the creation of this wiki).
The Destroyers are over-zealous editors. They come in and erase all the interesting bits because they are inconsistent with the rest of the work (i.e. normal reality) leaving a bland, boring world. The Foundation-verse without anomalous items would be boring and pointless to read, so it's boring and pointless to live in.

I really don't like the redaction here. From an in-universe point of view, there is no reason to redact any information because this note is either fiction, and shouldn't be redacted because false information can not compromise security, or comes from an alternate reality. There is no reason to protect information that by definition has no bearing on operations in this reality.
As a reader, it makes it hard to follow and sucks a lot of the juice out of the story. Instead of thinking about horrible situation the narrator is forced into, I spend all my time trying to work out what's under the black boxes. By the way, 343 is the only reasonable candidate for a male reality bender attemptign to preserve the status qou.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like you. You can come over to my house and fuck my sister. Not really, her fiance would hate that.

Keep it up and good luck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent, if rather brutal, critique. I like you.

I'll definitely give you the pacing issues; on re-reading, I do see some of what you were talking about: I'll work on consistent voice and pacing. Even after a few revisions to greatly improve flow and narrative (God help my verbosity) I did feel a breaking-of-flow to introduce concept and tone. Which, on reflection, was probably a mistake. I can already see how I could have implied more and said less, and will try to apply this in the future. This will probably improve pacing, though consistency of tone will probably only come with practice and finding each narrator's "voice" more clearly before writing.

Thanks for the input; I hope I'll hear from you again in the future (hopefully as my writing improves quite a bit, though this may be wishful thinking).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your pacing is bad and your writing is constipated. The story feels like sitting on the pot straining to get out a rock-hard dookie that has bound itself to the inside of your colon. There is no flow to it at all, no smooth transitions. Your story beats are all over the place: at the beginning of paragraphs, in the middle of paragraphs, at the end of paragraphs, and there's no rhyme or reason to where you put them. Your narrative skips all over the place, like someone whose line of thought has been disrupted, but the language used indicates someone in possession of their wits. You refer to the death of a character in cold, precise language, but the end indicates that the writer is traumatized enough to commit suicide.

In short, your story is scattered all over the place, and it has no impact because of it, like getting hit in the face with cotton candy. End result, boring story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First of all, thanks for the feedback! Tell me, what would "kick it up a notch" (in your opinion, of course; I know you can't please everyone)? You and TDM are the only ones that gave me a "meh", and I'd really like to hear what worked and what didn't. I love the site, and plan to contribute more in the future, so honest words that can help me focus on future efforts would probably help a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fair enough; I'll be the first to admit that it was a somewhat conservative first attempt. Mostly this was an exercise in style, formatting and to explore the idea of "The Destroyers" as something more than an overpowered alien threat.

If I may ask: what did you like? What left you feeling flat? What would you have done differently? I genuinely would like feedback, because I haven't stretched my writing muscles in years, and was wanting to get back into it. I'd actually love some real criticism that I could work from, and I'm comfortable enough in myself that I can handle honest talk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Concept is interesting. Execution is bland. Make it more interesting. Change your pacing. Vary your tempo. Do SOMETHING.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh… This isn't terrible, but it's pretty much just a generic, paint-by-the-numbers apocalypse tale with some kinda 'eh' Jung references. I like the genre, but really want something more from stories than what's here. Doesn't have to be a lot, but… Something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I'm pretty sure it's 343. Though with the author not telling, that means lots of tantalizing possibilities. Like what if it was 682?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Attribute Tags do not belong on tale pages. They are for SCPs only. I've removed them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's obviously Me losing my power and working in conjunction with SCP-329-j

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's SCP-181, wearing SCP-2100-J

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awww.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not telling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking it might be 343.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe SCP-846?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, it's totally 173.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, and -███ is here, but he's different, too. He's dying, and I think when he's gone, the rest of the site will be, too. I think he's protecting us, but it's killing him.

I choose to believe this is 682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suppose that's a good plot hook in the future, using the GOC as a foil, but I don't see them really going so far as true "scorched Earth" tactic… I think their leaders fit better as a "power-seeking" few that manipulate their lower ranks under the guise of "eliminating all occult threats" than really wanting a "magic-less" world.

As for Uganda, I pulled a random nation to give a more global feel for the Foundation's efforts rather than any specific reference. It was to show that the agent writing the doc was experienced and had "been around" to give him some flavor rather than have him or her say that "I'm so experienced and I've been in the shit". Nothing more than that, I'm afraid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm, but what if it was the GOC, who'd somehow figured out a way to rewrite reality to not just destroy all the anomalies, but make it so they never were? And they knew that it would result in the world becoming a dreary, dull place, but they though the price was worth it.

Oh, and the thing about Uganda. Is that in reference to some existing article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, gotcha. Now I'm gonna finally have to get around to playing the ME trilogy…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's from Mass Effect, the Reapers are giant semi-alien, semi-biological, and semi-artificial constructs that live in dark space and periodically exterminate sapient life so they don't get killed by their own creations. Indoctrination is a process that strips away a sapient creatures free will and creativity, prolonged exposure leads to mindless drones dependent on the Reaper for basic instructions.

A person suffering from Indoctrination will experience memory loss, lack of focus, loss of memories, loss of personality, depression, nightmares, and if the victim can resist long enough to retain concious control despite the mental damage done they will typically turn to suicide as the effects of a Reaper invasion/Indoctrination are irreversible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, go ahead! I'm flattered. Also, lazy. And, I don't know my way around the formatting yet, so I'd appreciate it…

Edit: Never mind, managed to figure it out. Thanks for reminding me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Author, gonna link this from Foundation Tales or do I need to do it for you? :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indoctrination? Is that an SCP reference I'm not remembering?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, you're excellent. Thanks taking the time to read my first attempt and comment on it. +1 Internets for you, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Freaking excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well… I may have added just a hint of SCP-447. For texture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! This is my first submission to the site, so it was a little nerve-wracking to hit that "post" button… So glad that people liked it.

item.title: Re: Eerie but effective
item.content:encoded:
We HAD a collab running a bit ago about aliens. And 1050 Forget the name.

What ever happened to that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not going to lie or joke, this is basically how I always imagined an Indoctrination victim would write their suicide note.

+1

item.title: Re: Eerie but effective
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I really liked the idea of the Destroyers from SCP-1050, and this tale is the result of trying to figure out a way to make them more menacing than most of the stuff the Foundation comes across.

In a way, it's sort of meta. The Destroyers are sort of the "cancer" that often inflicts boards like SCP Wiki; the magic sometimes just is gone, and what's left is just a pale, dead imitation of what used to be.

Not to say that SCP Wiki is dying! (I love this place, and they have excellent quality control.) I've just seen good writing boards die from their own success, and this is sort of a piece about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This…is really fucking good. +1'd hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article made with 100% natural awesomesauce. No artificial flavoring.

item.title: Eerie but effective
item.content:encoded: Good stuff. Depressing as all get-out, but apocalyptic stories usually are. Nicely done. Upvoted.
